title: qx contract (ADR-039 canonical form)
description: Meta-schema for a registry's .qx/contract.json. One canonical form: collection-generic descriptors (ADR-035 §0), the ADR-039 §2 scalar set (type ≠ widget). Governance is git-native (ADR-039 §6, revised): the ONLY in-file version is `format_version` (engine↔contract parse capability); contract identity is its content hash; who/when/why/approved is projected from the host review (PR reviews + merge commit), never stored here; effective-dating is commit-resolved. The tool's Rust types are the SSOT; this JSON Schema mirrors them for editor tooling + the CI meta-schema gate. CI validates the shipped contract against this file AND a fixture instance against the contract on every PR (no stale meta-schema, ADR-039 §4).

format_version.description: Engine↔contract parse capability (ADR-039 §6) — the ONLY in-file version. The tool holds the supported range as a const and refuses a format outside it. NOT a content/edit version (that is the contract's content hash, derived) and NOT governance (projected from the host review). Moves rarely — a format generation, not a content edit.

collections.description: The collection roster. The `parts` preset (code-owned floor) is mandatory and may be extended, never weakened (ADR-035 guardrail #1).

relations.description: Typed relations to other collections + graph rules (ADR-035 §1a). Beyond `components`, lands with the vocab collections.

open_properties.description: Tier-3 escape bag on/off (ADR-035 §1). Shape-checked only; regulated core fields are forbidden here (ADR-039 §5 demotion guard). Genuinely open / not-yet-schematized data lives here — NOT in a `json` field type (which does not exist).

default.description: One default scheme per registry; its bare value is a valid short form (no colon).

mintable.description: nano ids are minted; external schemes (udi:, gs1:) are asserted/imported.

type.description: ADR-039 §2 scalar set. The TYPE (data shape) — the widget is declared separately in `render` (type ≠ widget). There is no `json` type.

label.description: Display name, descriptor-owned (ADR-035 §1a). No hardcoded display strings in shells.

required_to_enter.description: Hard transition gate (ADR-039 §6): the entity cannot advance to <status> unless this field is present + valid. Closes the backdating gap.

meaningful_from.description: Downstream readers may TRUST this field once the entity reaches <status>. Documentation, not enforcement — use required_to_enter to gate.

on_unknown.description: Policy for a value not in the source set (enum/reference). Validation policy, survives the type/widget move.

values.description: enum facet: the closed value set.

closed.description: enum facet: reject / warn / accept unknown values.

unit.description: numeric facet — on integer/number/decimal (lifted off typeFields, ADR-039 §2).

scale.description: decimal facet: digits after the point.

key_field.description: reference facet: which target field the value matches (default: id).

display.description: reference facet: which target field shells render.

schema.description: object facet: REQUIRED nested field list or a $ref. An object without a schema would be a freeform-json backdoor (ADR-039 §2).

renderField.description: Chrome only — never a verdict. The widget a shell draws for this field.

suggest_from.description: combobox: a collection to suggest values from (free-text string field, accept-new).

label_fields.description: Which fields compose an entity's short label rendering.
